entry.title: "aesthetic surgery" - 新的结果
entry.content:
Current Concepts and Emerging Technologies in Aesthetic Outcome Assessment of Breast Reconstruction

D Lyu, FH Vollbach, I Mesas, B Fuchs, S Mert… - Aesthetic Surgery Journal, 2026
Background Aesthetic outcomes are a crucial determinant of the overall success of
breast reconstruction. Recently, aesthetic assessment has evolved from relying
mainly on subjective impressions to incorporating more quantitative methods …

R Gupta, SS Patel, AM Bhagwat, R Gupta, TY Xia… - Aesthetic Surgery Journal …, 2026
Background TikTok has risen to the forefront of social media platforms, becoming an
increasingly popular source for health information dissemination. The utilization of
glucagon-like-peptide-1 (GLP-1) agonists has resulted in a considerable increase in …

SY Park, M Kim, SE Yoon, JK Song, J Sloane… - Aesthetic Plastic Surgery, 2026
… This case series suggests that injectable PN therapy may be useful across
several postoperative scenarios relevant to aesthetic surgery: early ischemic change,
delayed epithelialization, wound separation, hypertrophic scarring, and localized …

N Rezania, KA Harmon, O La-Anyane, A Fritsch… - Aesthetic Surgery Journal, 2026
Background The SPY Fluorescence Imaging system enables intraoperative
visualization of microvascular perfusion. Recently, SPY has been integrated with
Quantitative Perfusion (QP) software to provide numerical assessment of relative …

… In addition, the commercialized nature of aesthetic surgery within the US may
further … as ‘Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery’ and ‘Aesthetic Surgery Journal’,
likely reflects their central … reporting that up to 73% of aesthetic surgery patients …

ZP Lorenc, M Gold, J Schlessinger, E Lain, S Smith… - Aesthetic Surgery Journal, 2026
Background CBL-514, a small-molecule injectable lipolysis agent that induces
adipocyte apoptosis, demonstrated clinically meaningful reductions in abdominal
subcutaneous fat by ultrasound in previous Phase 2 studies, with no observed …

[HTML] Safety of Augmentation Mastopexy in a Teaching Environment: Complication Analysis

F Marena, A Carrer Bortolini, A Maia Souto, F Hakme… - Aesthetic Surgery Journal …, 2026
… Furthermore, dedicated aesthetic surgery training 14 programs—particularly in
high-volume centers in Brazil—have demonstrated that residents 15 can attain high
levels of technical competence and procedural safety when directly involved 16 in …

A Calcium Silicate-derived Silicate Ion Solution Improves Fat Graft Retention and Promotes Angiogenesis: An Experimental Study

S Li, Z Yu, Z Zhang, Y Jiao, T Wang, Y Shi, Q Yang… - Aesthetic Surgery Journal, 2026
Background Autologous fat grafting is hampered by poor early vascularization,
leading to necrosis and unpredictable retention. Current pro-angiogenic strategies
face challenges like complexity and high cost. Calcium silicate (CS) has shown …

AT Nguyen, RA Dejenie, K Foote, R Nazerali - … , Reconstructive & Aesthetic Surgery, 2026
AbstractBackgroundOnline patient education materials (OPEMs) are increasingly
relied upon by patients seeking information on breast reconstruction. However, the
readability, quality, and cultural inclusivity of these resources remain unclear.MethodsFollowing …

Advancing Fat Graft Survival: From Adipose-Derived Stem Cell Mechanisms to Next-Generation Regenerative Strategies

X Ma, L Xu, J Zhang, X Wu, T Tao - Frontiers in Cell and Developmental Biology
… Autologous fat grafting is widely used in reconstructive and aesthetic surgery
because it is easy to harvest, well tolerated by the host, and produces natural-looking
results. However, unpredictable graft resorption—with reported volume retention …

entry.title: "botulinum toxin" - 新的结果
entry.content:
[HTML] Spinal mechanisms and feasibility of Dry Needling versus Botulinum Toxin Type A in post-stroke lower limb spasticity: A proof-of-concept randomized clinical trial …

C Pujol-Fuentes, B Eeckhaut, S Fernández Carnero… - PloS one, 2026
… Botulinum Toxin type A (BTX-A) and Dry Needling (DN) are treatments that
reduce spasticity, although Botulinum Toxin type A injections can cause adverse
effects. No studies have directly compared their effects at spinal, muscular, functional …

S Senthil, S Kumar, AW Shukla - Tremor and Other Hyperkinetic Movements, 2026
… Although there are reports linking CD to cervical spine complications, to date,
most published data has lacked detailed information on the timeline for the onset of
cervical spine complications following a diagnosis of CD and the potential impact of …

[HTML] Cheng Hao Tien 1, 8 Min Chi Chiu 1 Yi Ling Shen 2 Yun Chen Ko 2, 7

JJ Lee
… , and injection-based therapies including platelet-rich plasma, botulinum toxin A,
corticosteroids, autologous blood, and local anaesthetics. Across short-, medium-,
and long-term follow- up, botulinum toxin A provided the greatest short-term …

Urge Urinary Incontinence: Surgical Management

N Boxall, M Pakzad - … and Gynecological Women's Health: Prevention and …, 2026
… Botulinum Toxin Botulinum toxin is a neurotoxin produced by Clostridium
botulinum. Of the seven subtypes, subtype A has the longest duration of action, and
thus is the most useful clinically. It acts by cleaving the SNAP-25 attachment protein …

A Esquenazi, TH Wein, M Verduzco-Gutierrez… - Advances in Therapy, 2026
… Some people with upper limb spasticity receive botulinum toxin A (BoNT-A)
injections to help relax the affected muscles. BoNT-A injections work for a period, but
retreatment is required when the effects start to wear off. AbobotulinumtoxinA and …

JRC Miller, N Cox, A Minocha, L Gallagher, A Kennedy - Strabismus, 2026
… More widespread use may help to expand the eligible patient population,
allowing access to corrective surgery for those who would otherwise only be eligible
for botulinum toxin in an outpatient setting. We also demonstrate the potential to …

PS Tripthi, R Kumar - Journal of Maxillofacial and Oral Surgery, 2026

toxin-A may benefit refractory cases. However, several drugs, including
proparacaine and tocainide, were found inappropriate for TN treatment. Although …

LC Panegossi, MF Arabe Filho, TS Bello, PJL Paz… - Arquivo Brasileiro de …, 2026
Botulism is a toxi-infection caused by the ingestion of preformed Clostridium
botulinum neurotoxins from decaying carcasses or putrefied food. In cats, botulism is
a rare clinical condition. A two-year-old male crossbreed cat, raised in a farm …

M Gordon, A de Geus, M Boruta, M Banasiuk… - Journal of Pediatric …, 2026

children with refractory constipation,80 all … botulinum toxin injections in the anal

E PARK, YL Kim - 2026

Dermatol Res. … Botulinum toxin applications in the lower face and neck: A
comprehensive review. J. cosmet… Treatment of gummy smile using botulinum toxin …

structural conservation while achieving aesthetic refinement and functional
